title: upstream podman image should have dnsname plugin installed and enabled
body:
/kind feature

**Description**

A popular feature is to use a dns server to facilitate name lookups between containers in a custom network. This is possible with some manual setup, but not in the quay.io/podman/stable:v3.3.0 image.
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes/No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL - it sounds like we might not be installing recommended packages? `podman-plugins` might not be in the image?

@mattymo @mheon we keep the image pretty darn lean, perhaps too lean?

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/contrib/podmanimage/stable/Dockerfile#L14
```
RUN dnf -y update; rpm --restore shadow-utils 2>/dev/null; \
yum -y install podman fuse-overlayfs --exclude container-selinux; \
rm -rf /var/cache /var/log/dnf* /var/log/yum.*
```

I'd be find with adding podman-plugins if that makes sense and doesn't bloat the image too much.  @rhatdan thoughts?
I'm mistaken. I used contrib/podmanimage/upstream/Dockerfile and it doesn't include plugins. The stable image does include dnsname plugin binary. I will reopen if I find issues with it
I'm opening a PR to add podman-plugins installed in the upstream image
Makes sense that we should ship it in the image.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
